title: ranunculus dissectus subsp. glabrescens
intro: Ranunculus dissectus subsp. glabrescens, also known as the dissected buttercup, is a plant species in the buttercup family that is native to North America. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, specifically its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been used to treat a variety of health issues, including wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory problems. The health benefits of Ranunculus dissectus subsp. glabrescens include its ability to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu. Herbal preparations of the plant are typically made by infusing the leaves and flowers in water or oil, and can be used in topical applications, teas, or as a supplement to support overall health and well-being.
